extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which fires when all 8 booleans are true, including hyperkalemia. rec_drug_b is set true by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) whenever hyperkalemia is true. Since hyperkalemia is one of the conjuncts required for rec_drug_a, any input that fires rec_a_from_conjunction necessarily has hyperkalemia=true, which fires rec_b_from_trigger. The two rules write to different outputs (rec_drug_a vs rec_drug_b), so per-output priority resolution does not prevent either from firing; no rule sets either output back to false. Thus setting all 8 conjuncts true forces both outputs true, violating mutual exclusion. heart_failure is irrelevant to the violation (it only affects rec_drug_c); set to false here. Counterexample verified: rec_a_from_conjunction -> rec_drug_a=true; rec_b_from_trigger (hyperkalemia) -> rec_drug_b=true; rec_drug_c defaults false. rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b = true.
